name: Input
description: Single-line text entry field. Pairs with a visible label and optional helper text.

props.name: id
props.description: Links the input to its <label>. Must be unique per page.

props.name: label
props.description: Visible label text. Always required — do not use placeholder as a substitute.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Hint text shown when empty. Supplementary — not a label replacement.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Supplementary hint below the field. Hidden when errorMessage is present.

props.name: errorMessage
props.description: Validation error. When set, input switches to error state and this message replaces helperText.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks field as required; adds aria-required and visual indicator

props.name: type
props.description: HTML input type (text, email, password, number, search, url, tel)

variants.name: default
variants.description: Normal state — border default, subtle background.

variants.name: error
variants.description: Validation failed — red border, error message displayed below.

states.name: focus
states.description: Active — focus ring, border color shifts to focus blue

states.name: error
states.description: Validation failed — red border, error message visible

states.name: error-focus
states.description: Error state with focus ring — focus ring uses error color

states.name: disabled
states.description: Greyed out — interaction prevented, opacity reduced

states.name: filled
states.description: Has a value — no visual change; placeholder hidden

do: Always provide a visible label — use the label prop, not placeholder alone.
Use helperText to set user expectations before they interact (e.g. 'Must be 8+ characters').
Use errorMessage after validation, not before — don't show errors before the user has had a chance to fill in the field.
Use type='email', type='password', etc. for correct mobile keyboard and browser auto-fill.
Mark required fields with required prop and communicate requirements in the label or helperText.
dont: Don't use placeholder as a label — it disappears on input and fails contrast requirements.
Don't show an error before the field has been interacted with.
Don't rely on color alone to communicate error state — the error message text is essential.
Don't disable a field to prevent editing when readonly is more appropriate.
Don't use for multi-line text — use a <textarea> instead.

keyboard: Tab — moves focus to the input
aria: aria-required='true' when required prop is set
aria-describedby pointing to helperText or errorMessage element id
<label htmlFor={id}> must always be present and visible

examples.label: Basic input
examples.description: Controlled input with label and placeholder

examples.label: With helper text
examples.code: <Input id='password' label='Password' type='password' helperText='Must be at least 8 characters' />

examples.label: Error state
examples.code: <Input id='username' label='Username' value='ab' errorMessage='Username must be at least 3 characters' />
